title: Potato site-specific fertilizer schedules — upland vs Jaffna vs rice-based vs Kalpitiya
body: HORDI publishes four distinct potato fertilizer schedules calibrated by soil type and cropping system. (1) Upland (Nuwara Eliya, Badulla) + Jaffna calcic red latosols: Basal Urea 55 + TSP 270 + MOP 125 kg/ha, then Urea 110 at 2 weeks, then Urea 165 + MOP 125 at 3–4 weeks. (2) Rice-based cropping system (rice+potato rotation in Polonnaruwa / Ampara): same Urea schedule but MOP is lower — 85 kg/ha basal and 85 kg/ha at 3–4 weeks, because the rice-paddy soils already carry residual K. (3) Kalpitiya regosols: TSP 270 kg/ha basal only, skip MOP basal because regosols leach K too fast; top-dressing varies by week. Picking the wrong schedule costs 20–30% yield — ask the local DS officer for a soil test before starting a commercial 2,000 kg/ha seed-tuber plot.
quote: Uplands (Nuwara Eliya, Badulla) & Jaffna calcic red latosols: Basal 55/270/125; 2 weeks 110/–/–; 3–4 weeks 165/–/125. Rice-based cropping system: MOP adjusted to 85 kg/ha (basal) and 85 kg/ha (3–4 weeks). Kalpitiya regosols: Basal TSP 270 kg/ha only.
